mechanism_narrative: A sustained 3-month decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 10% increase in the housing market (HOUST) over the next 5 trading days signals a surge in consumer spending, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in periods with HOUST growth > 10% are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p < 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 5% decline in industrial production (INDPRO) over the next 60 trading days signals a supply-side shock that compresses profit margins, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window, or 60-day forward returns in periods with INDPRO decline > 5% are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 0.5% increase in the unemployment rate (UNRATE) over the next 20 trading days signals a tightening labor market that drives up wages, compressing profit margins and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in periods with UNRATE growth > 0.5% are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p < 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 10% increase in the housing market (HOUST) and a 5% increase in the retail sales (RSAFS) over the next 5 trading days signals a surge in consumer spending, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in periods with HOUST growth > 10% and RSAFS growth > 5% are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p < 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 1% increase in the federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) over the next 20 trading days signals a tightening monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in tightening periods are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 5% decline in industrial production (INDPRO) and a 10% decline in the housing market (HOUST) over the next 60 trading days signals a supply-side shock that compresses profit margins, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window, or 60-day forward returns in periods with INDPRO decline > 5% and HOUST decline > 10% are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 0.5% increase in the unemployment rate (UNRATE) and a 1% increase in the labor market (PAYEMS) over the next 20 trading days signals a tightening labor market that drives up wages, compressing profit margins and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in periods with UNRATE growth > 0.5% and PAYEMS growth > 1% are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p < 0.05).
